content: [DECISION] Picked Clerk over Auth0 for Navi auth. Auth0 quote was $2400/mo at our MAU projection; Clerk Pro is $99 + $0.02/MAU. Priya and Marcus signed off. NextAuth ruled out due to SOC2 audit log gaps.
[FACT] Clerk session TTL defaults to 7 days inactivity, 30 days absolute. Navi SPV portal overrides to 24h inactivity / 8h absolute for LP-facing routes per compliance ask from Dana.
[MEETING] 10/14 standup: Priya flagged that magic-link flow breaks on Outlook SafeLinks (prefetch burns the token). Action: switch to 6-digit OTP for enterprise LPs, keep magic-link for retail.

[PLAN] SOC2 Type I prep Q1: export Clerk audit logs to Datadog daily, add MFA enforcement for admin role, document session policies in /docs/security/auth.md, and get pen test scheduled with Cobalt by Feb 15.
[COMPLETION] Shipped Clerk integration behind feature flag clerk_auth_v1 on 10/22. Migrated 847 existing users via Clerk's bulk import API. Legacy NextAuth routes return 410 after 11/05 cutover.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said Clerk supports SAML on Pro plan — wrong. SAML requires Enterprise tier ($500/mo min). Updated budget doc. Fortress Capital onboarding blocked until we upgrade or use OIDC workaround.
[HANDOFF] Marcus out next week. Active work: webhook handler for user.created is dropping events when Clerk retries within 200ms (idempotency key bug). Branch fix/clerk-webhook-dedup, see PR #412.

[DECISION] Going passwordless-only for Navi. No password field at all. Magic-link primary, OTP fallback for Outlook/corp mail. Decided against password option after reviewing NIST 800-63B and investor UX survey (n=34, 79% preferred link).

[FACT] Clerk webhook signing secret rotates every 90 days. Stored in Doppler under navi/prod/clerk_webhook_secret. Rotation runbook: update Doppler, redeploy api-gateway, verify /api/webhooks/clerk returns 200 on test event.

[MEETING] 10/28 SOC2 kickoff with Vanta + auditor (Prescient Assurance). Auditor wants evidence of session revocation on role change. Clerk's sessions.revoke API covers this; need to wire into our RBAC update path.

[PLAN] Migrate admin panel from magic-link to hardware key (WebAuthn) by end of Q1. Clerk supports passkeys natively. Scope: 12 internal admins, Yubikey 5C NFC distributed, fallback OTP for lost-key recovery.

[COMPLETION] Finished audit log pipeline: Clerk → Svix webhook → Kafka topic auth.events → Snowflake raw_auth_logs. 7-year retention per SOC2. Validated with Dana, backfilled 60 days of history from Clerk API.

[CORRECTION] Session TTL config was applied in dev only; prod still used 7-day default for two weeks (10/22–11/05). No breach, but logged as SOC2 finding. Fixed via Clerk JWT template overrides, added Terraform check.

[HANDOFF] Passing Clerk dashboard owner from Marcus to Priya. Billing email updated to finance@navi.vc. 2FA on Clerk account is Marcus's Yubikey — Priya needs to add hers before 12/01 then we revoke Marcus's.

[DECISION] Keep NextAuth on the marketing site (navi.vc) — only the app subdomain (app.navi.vc) uses Clerk. Avoids paying Clerk MAU for blog/newsletter signups. Reviewed with Marcus 11/02, documented in ADR-0031.

[DECISION] Going with Stripe Connect Express accounts for LPs instead of Custom. Priya argued Custom gives more control but the KYC lift isn't worth it for our volume (~120 LPs). Express handles onboarding UI and we keep liability lower.

[FACT] Stripe test mode webhooks for payment_intent.succeeded fire ~2s after confirmation in our staging env. The Connect events (transfer.created, payout.paid) lag 4-8s. Don't assume ordering between the two streams.

[PLAN] Carry distribution pipeline: compute GP split in Postgres view v_carry_allocations, batch into Stripe Transfer objects with idempotency_key = f"carry:{fund_id}:{period}:{gp_id}". Dry-run against test clock before prod run Nov 15.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said idempotency keys expire after 24h. Wrong - Stripe keeps them for 24h of request replay protection but the underlying object persists. Safe to reuse same key on retry even days later.

[MEETING] Standup with Raj, Priya, Tomas. Blocker: K-1 generation depends on carry amounts being finalized, but audit wants to review before distribution. Decision: generate draft K-1s from projected splits, regenerate post-distribution.

[COMPLETION] Shipped webhook handler at /stripe/connect/events behind signing secret verification. Handles 14 event types, writes to navi_webhook_log table, dispatches to handlers via event.type switch. Deployed to staging 10/22.

[DECISION] Idempotency key format locked: f"navi:{entity}:{id}:{action}:{attempt_bucket}" where attempt_bucket is day-granularity. Avoids the retry-after-midnight collision Tomas hit in the Oct 14 test run.

[FACT] K-1 box 11 (other income) is where carried interest lands for GPs. Box 1 for LPs reflects their share of fund ordinary income. Confirmed with Grant Thornton contact — they'll sign off on the template by EOQ.

[HANDOFF] Raj taking over K-1 PDF generation. Stack: Jinja template -> WeasyPrint -> S3 with KMS. Template lives in reports/k1/2024.html.j2. Test fixtures in tests/fixtures/k1_golden/. Ping me before touching the box 20 codes.

[PLAN] Next sprint: replace manual CSV upload for LP contributions with Stripe Checkout sessions. Each session metadata carries fund_id and commitment_id so webhook can reconcile. Targeting Nov 8 for internal dogfood.

[CORRECTION] My earlier estimate that Stripe Connect fees would be 0.25% was using the old platform pricing. Actual is 2% on payouts to Express accounts plus $2 monthly per active account. Reran model — still cheaper than Modern Treasury.

[MEETING] Sync with Stripe solutions eng (Dana). Confirmed test clocks work with Connect transfers now — previously broken. Also: use Stripe-Account header for all Connect API calls, not the account param. Saved notes in Notion/navi/stripe-call-1024.

[COMPLETION] Idempotency key audit done. Ran script over last 90 days of navi_stripe_requests: 0 duplicate charges, 2 duplicate transfer attempts both correctly rejected by Stripe with same response body. Posted summary in #navi-eng.

[HANDOFF] Priya owns the K-1 reconciliation report going forward. Inputs: carry_allocations table + stripe_transfers view. Expected delta is zero; any non-zero rows page via PagerDuty service navi-finops. Runbook in docs/navi/k1-recon.md.

[FACT] Stripe test-mode webhook signing secret is different from live-mode. Stored in AWS Secrets Manager at navi/stripe/webhook_secret_test and .../webhook_secret_live. The handler picks based on STRIPE_ENV env var, defaulting to test.

[DECISION] Went with soft deletes on deals table using valid_from/valid_to timestamps instead of a deleted_at flag. Priya argued it lets us reconstruct LP allocation history at any point for audit without a separate events table.

[PLAN] Migration 0042 will add valid_to TIMESTAMPTZ NULL to term_sheets, backfill with NULL for current rows, then add partial unique index on (deal_id) WHERE valid_to IS NULL to enforce one active term sheet per deal.
[FACT] The deals table currently holds 3,412 rows across 7 funds. Navi III alone accounts for 1,180. Avg 2.3 term sheet revisions per deal, so term_sheets is around 7.8k rows pre-migration.
[MEETING] Standup 10/14 with Raj, Priya, Dan: agreed lp_allocations must reference term_sheet_id not deal_id, so allocations get versioned with the sheet. Raj to draft ADR by Thursday.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said valid_to defaults to 'infinity'::timestamptz. Wrong — we're using NULL to mean currently-active. Postgres infinity breaks our ORM (Prisma) which maps it to an invalid Date.

[COMPLETION] Migration 0042 applied to staging. 7,812 term_sheets rows backfilled, partial unique index built in 4.2s. Verified one active sheet per deal via COUNT(*) GROUP BY deal_id HAVING > 1 returned zero.

[DECISION] Rejected using tstzrange for validity columns. Dan prototyped it but exclusion constraints with GIST added 60ms to inserts under pgbench. Two plain columns + trigger is enough for our 20 writes/min.

[HANDOFF] Handing term_sheets migration rollout to Dan. Prod window is Saturday 11/2 02:00 UTC. Runbook in notion/navi/migrations/0042.md, includes pg_dump snapshot step and revert SQL if partial index build stalls.

[FACT] lp_allocations.amount_cents is BIGINT NOT NULL CHECK (amount_cents > 0). Sum per term_sheet_id must equal term_sheets.commitment_cents — enforced via deferred constraint trigger check_allocation_sum.
[PLAN] Phase 2: add valid_to to lp_allocations so we can model mid-quarter reallocations. Blocked until 0042 lands because allocations FK moves from deal_id to term_sheet_id first.

[MEETING] Review with legal ops 10/21: Jen confirmed audit requirement is 7-year retention of any LP allocation ever signed. valid_to=NULL + never-hard-delete policy satisfies it. No separate archive table needed.

[CORRECTION] The check_allocation_sum trigger was NOT deferrable as originally shipped in migration 0038. Fixed in 0041 — set CONSTRAINT ... DEFERRABLE INITIALLY DEFERRED so batch inserts from the importer work.
[COMPLETION] Backfilled valid_from on deals table from created_at for 3,412 rows. Took 1.1s on prod replica. Added NOT NULL constraint after. Priya signed off on the data spot-check across Navi I–IV.

[DECISION] Chose to keep term_sheets.status enum (draft, signed, superseded, void) even with valid_to. Status captures intent, valid_to captures temporal truth. Dan flagged they can disagree briefly during countersign.

[HANDOFF] Raj taking over the Prisma schema regeneration after 0042. Needs to update allocation resolver to filter WHERE valid_to IS NULL by default, and add includeHistorical flag for the audit view at /deals/:id/history.

[DECISION] Went with Next.js 14 App Router over Pages Router for the LP portal relaunch. Priya argued the streaming SSR wins outweigh the Sanity plugin refactor cost. Decision locked in at Tuesday's arch review.
[FACT] Sanity project ID is fdry-lp-2024, dataset 'production'. Studio deployed at studio.foundry.capital. CMS seats: 6 (3 marketing, 2 IR, 1 engineering admin).

[PLAN] Phase 1 (Oct 7-18): rebrand tokens + Tailwind config. Phase 2 (Oct 21-Nov 1): investor dashboard wireframes wired to Sanity. Phase 3 (Nov 4-15): mobile QA on iPhone SE, Pixel 7, iPad mini. Launch target Nov 20.

[MEETING] Stand-up with Jordan, Priya, Mei. Jordan flagged that the new Söhne font license only covers 2 domains; need to exclude staging.foundry.capital or buy expanded license (~$1.2k). Mei to confirm with legal.
[DECISION] Dashboards will use Recharts, not Visx. Reason: Recharts handles the quarterly NAV bar+line combo with less boilerplate and the LP audience doesn't need Visx-level customization.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said Clerk for auth on the investor portal. Reversed — using NextAuth with Okta SAML because Foundry IT already provisions LP identities through Okta. Clerk removed from package.json.
[COMPLETION] Shipped rebrand token pass: new palette (ink #0B1220, sand #F4EFE6, signal #E8553D) wired through Tailwind theme. All 14 marketing pages pass Lighthouse contrast checks.

[HANDOFF] Handing Sanity schema work to Mei. Current schemas: fund, portfolioCompany, lpUpdate, teamMember. Pending: quarterlyReport schema with PDF asset + NAV fields. Branch: feature/sanity-quarterly.
[FACT] Mobile-first breakpoints fixed at sm:640, md:768, lg:1024, xl:1280. Hero section collapses to single column under 768. Dashboard tables switch to card layout under 640 per Priya's spec.

[DECISION] Investor dashboard data will be fetched server-side via Next route handlers hitting internal NAV API, not directly from Sanity. Sanity only holds narrative content (commentary, fund thesis).

[MEETING] Reviewed Figma v3 with Diana (brand) and two LPs from Accel-backed fund. Feedback: reduce gradient intensity on hero, add explicit 'Accredited Investor' gate before /portal. Diana updating by Friday.
[COMPLETION] Okta SAML integration live in staging. Test LP account (test-lp@foundry.capital) successfully accesses /portal/dashboard. Session cookie set to 8h, refresh via Okta token endpoint.
[PLAN] Analytics: PostHog for product events on /portal, Plausible for marketing pages. No GA4 — Jordan vetoed due to LP privacy concerns. Cookie banner only surfaces for PostHog consent.
[CORRECTION] Breakpoint sm was incorrectly set to 480 in initial Tailwind config — pushed fix to 640 to match Priya's spec in episode above. Commit 7a3f21c.

[HANDOFF] Priya taking over mobile QA from me Nov 4. BrowserStack credentials in 1Password vault 'Foundry-LP'. Known issue: iOS Safari 16 clips the sticky nav on /funds/[slug] — needs -webkit-sticky fallback.

[DECISION] Picked SendGrid over Postmark for the Foundry newsletter stack. Priya ran the numbers: at projected 45k weekly sends, SendGrid Pro at $89/mo beats Postmark's $150. Deliverability parity was confirmed via a 500-email test batch to seed inboxes.

[PLAN] Week of Oct 14: wire SendGrid dynamic templates for the Monday macro briefing, migrate the 12k existing Mailchimp subscribers via CSV export, then cut over DNS. Marcus owns the template port, I handle the segmentation logic and unsubscribe endpoint.

[FACT] SendGrid API key lives in 1Password vault 'Foundry-Prod' under SENDGRID_API_KEY. Scoped to Mail Send + Template Engine only. Rotate quarterly. The sandbox key (SENDGRID_TEST_KEY) points to a separate subuser so staging blasts don't touch the production reputation.

[MEETING] Standup 10/16 with Priya, Marcus, Dana. Decided segmentation v1 uses three cohorts: 'pro_traders' (paid), 'free_weekly' (opt-in), 'churned_30d'. Dana raised GDPR concern about the churned cohort — parked for legal review next Tuesday.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said SPF record should include 'sendgrid.net'. Wrong — correct include is 'include:sendgrid.net' inside the existing TXT, and we also need the specific 'u12345.wl.sendgrid.net' CNAMEs for domain authentication. Fixed in Cloudflare at 2:14pm.

[COMPLETION] DKIM set up for foundry.news. Two CNAMEs (s1._domainkey, s2._domainkey) pointing to SendGrid's selectors, verified green in the SendGrid Sender Auth dashboard. Mail-tester.com score jumped from 7.1 to 9.8/10.

[HANDOFF] Marcus taking over the unsubscribe flow. Current state: /api/unsubscribe endpoint accepts the signed token from the List-Unsubscribe header, flips subscriber.status to 'unsubscribed' in Postgres. Still TODO: one-click POST per RFC 8058 and the preference center UI.

[FACT] The weekly macro briefing ships every Monday 06:30 ET via a Vercel cron hitting /api/send-briefing. It pulls the latest MDX from the 'briefings' repo, renders with SendGrid dynamic template d-8f2a91c4, and batches 1000 recipients per API call to stay under rate limits.

[DECISION] Storing unsubscribe tokens as HMAC-SHA256 of subscriber_id + a server secret, not JWTs. Shorter URLs, no expiry needed for unsubscribe links, and we can verify without a DB lookup. Secret is UNSUB_SIGNING_KEY, rotated only on suspected compromise.

[PLAN] Segmentation v2 roadmap: add behavioral tags based on open/click events from SendGrid's Event Webhook. Target cohorts like 'opened_last_3' and 'clicked_premium_cta'. Need to stand up /api/sendgrid-webhook with signature verification before we can populate any of this.

[CORRECTION] I told Dana the List-Unsubscribe header was optional for Gmail bulk sender compliance. That's wrong as of Feb 2024 — Google requires both List-Unsubscribe and List-Unsubscribe-Post: List-Unsubscribe=One-Click for senders over 5k/day. We're at 12k so it's mandatory.

[COMPLETION] Migrated all 12,043 Mailchimp subscribers into the Foundry Postgres 'subscribers' table. 47 bounced during the validation pass (hard bounces from Mailchimp's own suppression list) and were marked status='suppressed' so we never attempt delivery.

[MEETING] Deliverability review with SendGrid rep Jordan on 10/22. Our domain reputation sits at 94. Jordan flagged that the 'churned_30d' re-engagement campaign had a 0.4% spam complaint rate — above the 0.1% threshold. Agreed to suppress anyone dormant 90+ days.

[HANDOFF] Dana picking up the preference center at foundry.news/preferences. Spec: let subscribers toggle 'weekly macro', 'breaking alerts', 'product updates' independently instead of all-or-nothing unsubscribe. Schema change already shipped — subscription_prefs JSONB column on subscribers.

[FACT] DMARC policy on foundry.news is currently p=quarantine, pct=25, with rua=mailto:dmarc@foundry.news aggregating reports via Postmark's DMARC Digests. Plan is to move to p=reject once we've seen four clean weekly reports in a row — two down, two to go.

[DECISION] Going with Airflow 2.7 over Prefect for the catalyst pipeline. Maya pushed for Prefect but the team's existing Airflow expertise and our Astronomer contract tipped it. Revisit in Q3 if pain points accumulate.

[PLAN] Backfill strategy: chunk by trading day, 30 days per DAG run, max 4 parallel workers. Start from 2019-01-01 for NYSE symbols, 2020-06 for crypto. Estimated 18 hours wall-clock on the m5.2xlarge workers.

[FACT] Postgres target is catalyst_prod on RDS, db.r6g.xlarge, 500GB gp3. Primary table market_ticks is partitioned monthly by ts. Current row count ~2.1B. Owner: data-platform team, oncall rotates weekly.

[DECISION] Dedup key is (symbol, ts, source) with ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING on insert. Considered MERGE but insert-only is simpler and Kafka already guarantees at-least-once. Raj signed off in the 9/12 review.

[MEETING] 9/12 pipeline review with Raj, Maya, Chen. Agreed: dedup via unique constraint, backfill in reverse chronological, alert on >5% null price rows. Action items assigned in Linear CAT-214 through CAT-219.

[CORRECTION] Earlier I said dedup was on (symbol, ts). Actually the constraint includes source too since we pull from both Polygon and IEX and timestamps can collide across venues. Fixed in migration 0043.
[COMPLETION] Merged the ingest_market_ticks DAG to main (PR #412). Backfill task group, dedup upsert, and Slack alerting all wired up. Deployed to Astronomer dev; prod rollout scheduled for Thursday.

[FACT] Polygon API rate limit on our plan: 100 req/sec. We batch 50k ticks per request using their flat file S3 endpoint instead, which sidesteps the per-request limit entirely. Credentials in AWS Secrets Manager under catalyst/polygon.

[PLAN] Phase 2: add IEX DEEP feed as secondary source for redundancy. Chen scoping effort, estimate 2 sprints. Will require schema addition for book-level fields and a new source='iex_deep' enum value.

[HANDOFF] Passing backfill monitoring to Chen while I'm on PTO 9/23-9/30. Runbook at confluence/catalyst/backfill-runbook. If DAG fails on retry 3, check RDS connection pool first, then Polygon S3 credentials. Page me only for data loss.

[CORRECTION] Initial backfill estimate of 18 hours was wrong — forgot Polygon flat files are gzipped and decompression is CPU-bound on workers. Actual wall-clock ran 31 hours. Updated the runbook and the capacity doc.

[DECISION] Using Airflow TaskFlow API for the DAG instead of classic PythonOperator. Cleaner XCom handling for the chunk boundaries and Maya prefers it. One caveat: dynamic task mapping needs Airflow >=2.5 which we already have.

[COMPLETION] Backfill finished at 04:12 UTC, 2.1B rows loaded, 847M duplicates rejected by the unique constraint (expected, from Kafka replay). Validation query against Polygon reference counts matches within 0.003%.

[MEETING] Standup 9/18: Chen flagged Airflow scheduler lag spiking during backfill. Agreed to bump scheduler to 2 replicas and move backfill DAG to a dedicated pool 'backfill_heavy' with 4 slots. Maya to implement.

[FACT] market_ticks has 6 indexes: PK on id, unique on (symbol,ts,source), btree on symbol, brin on ts, btree on source, partial idx on symbol where source='polygon'. The brin index is critical for range scans over partitions.
[DECISION] Going with Sonnet 3.5 as default for transcript summarization; Opus only triggered when confidence score from router < 0.6. Saves ~4x on token costs per Priya's cost model.
[FACT] Average Q3 earnings transcript is 48k tokens. Sonnet handles full context fine, but latency jumps from 12s to 31s above 60k tokens. Measured on 200-call sample.
[PLAN] Next sprint: migrate RAG layer from Pinecone to Neo4j vector index. Rohan owns schema design, wants (Company)-[:REPORTED]->(Quarter)-[:CONTAINS]->(Chunk) with embeddings on Chunk nodes.
[MEETING] Standup 10/14: Dana flagged that Opus hallucinates ticker symbols ~3% of the time on small-caps. Decided to add a post-hoc validator against the Polygon ticker list.
[CORRECTION] Earlier claim that Sonnet was cheaper per summary was wrong when retries included. With 8% retry rate, effective cost is $0.071/card vs $0.058 for Haiku+Opus cascade.
[COMPLETION] Shipped the summary card schema v2 with fields: headline, guidance_delta, analyst_sentiment, top_3_risks. Backfilled 1,240 prior cards overnight via batch API.
[HANDOFF] Passing Neo4j ingestion pipeline to Marcus. Key gotcha: transcripts from Seeking Alpha have speaker labels in bold HTML, AlphaSense uses plain text. Parser branches on source_id.
[DECISION] Dropping sentence-window retrieval in favor of parent-child chunking. 512-token children, 2048-token parents. Recall@5 went from 0.71 to 0.84 on our 50-question eval set.
[FACT] Opus 4 on guidance extraction: 94.2% F1 vs Sonnet 3.5 at 89.1% on the NVDA/AMD/INTC test set. Gap widens to 11 points on non-US filers per Lin's eval run last Thursday.
[PLAN] Q1 roadmap: add prompt caching for the system prompt (17k tokens of formatting examples). Anthropic docs suggest ~90% cost reduction on cached portion, break-even after 3 calls.
[MEETING] Arch review with Sameer: concern that Neo4j won't scale past 10M chunks. Proposed fallback: keep Neo4j for graph traversal, push raw vectors to Qdrant. Revisit at 5M chunk milestone.
[COMPLETION] Router model deployed — classifies transcript complexity into {simple, standard, complex} and picks Haiku/Sonnet/Opus. 78% of calls now route to Sonnet, 14% Haiku, 8% Opus.
[CORRECTION] The 94.2% F1 number for Opus was on cleaned transcripts. On raw Seeking Alpha HTML with speaker tags intact, Opus drops to 88.7%. Need to preprocess before benchmarking.
[HANDOFF] Dana taking over eval harness. Runs nightly against golden set of 50 transcripts in s3://catalyst-eval/golden/. Slack alerts to #catalyst-alerts if F1 drops >2 points week-over-week.
[DECISION] Using text-embedding-3-large (3072 dim) for Neo4j vectors despite the storage cost. Tested against voyage-finance-2; OpenAI won on our domain by 6 points NDCG@10.

[DECISION] Picked SvelteKit over Next.js for Combinator frontend. Maya argued the smaller bundle size (~40KB vs 90KB) matters for the strategy visualization canvas. Deciding factor was Svelte's reactive stores mapping cleanly to combinator state.

[PLAN] Week of Nov 4: scaffold SvelteKit app, wire up Tailwind, stub out /mix and /strategies routes. Raj handles auth integration, Maya builds the combinator graph view. Target demo-ready build by Nov 15.
[FACT] Combinator MVP runs on Fly.io in the iad region. Two shared-cpu-1x machines behind the built-in load balancer. Postgres is a Fly managed cluster, 1GB volume. Monthly cost sits around $18.

[MEETING] Nov 6 sync with Priya, Raj, Maya. Debated Vercel vs Fly.io for hosting. Priya pushed Vercel for DX but Raj flagged the $20/seat pricing scaling poorly past 5 devs. Tabled final call until Thursday.

[DECISION] Going with Fly.io over Vercel for Combinator hosting. Cheaper for our sustained traffic profile and we already have Fly CLI muscle memory from the Loom project. Vercel stays as fallback if edge functions become critical.

[PLAN] Beta cohort target: 12 testers from the Recurse Center strategy-games channel plus 3 friends-of-team. Onboarding via Loom walkthrough + shared Notion feedback doc. Kickoff Nov 22, two-week structured feedback window.
[CORRECTION] Earlier note said beta cohort was 15 testers. Actual count is 12 Recurse + 3 external = 15 slots but only 11 confirmed as of Nov 18. Fixing the README and the tracking sheet.

[COMPLETION] SvelteKit scaffold merged to main. Tailwind configured with the Combinator palette (indigo-600 primary, slate-900 bg). Routes /mix, /strategies, /dashboard stubbed. CI green on GitHub Actions. Took Maya ~2 days.

[HANDOFF] Raj handing auth work to Devin. Clerk is wired up to SvelteKit via the official adapter, session cookies working locally. Remaining: configure production Clerk instance, add org-level permissions for beta testers. Slack thread #combinator-auth has context.

[FACT] Combinator uses Clerk for auth, free tier (up to 10K MAUs). JWT tokens verified server-side in SvelteKit hooks.server.ts. We chose Clerk over Auth0 mainly because Auth0's free tier capped at 7K MAUs and their SvelteKit docs were stale.

[MEETING] Nov 13 beta planning call. Priya proposed tiered rollout: 5 testers Nov 22, full 15 by Dec 1. Maya pushed back, wanted all 15 at once for concentrated feedback. Compromise: all 15 on Nov 22 but weekly office hours Wednesdays.

[COMPLETION] Fly.io production deploy live at combinator.fly.dev. HTTPS via Fly's auto-cert, Postgres migrated with Prisma. Smoke tests pass. Raj deployed at 4:17pm, zero downtime rollout confirmed via healthcheck logs.

[DECISION] Beta testers get a custom Clerk organization with the 'beta' role. Gives us feature flag targeting without building our own system. Devin implemented the has({role: 'beta'}) check in the /strategies loader.

[HANDOFF] Maya off next week, Priya takes over combinator graph view. Current state: d3-force layout working, node drag broken on Safari (known WebKit pointer event bug). See PR #47 and the bug note in docs/known-issues.md.

[PLAN] Post-beta metrics to track: time-to-first-mix, number of strategies saved per user, Wednesday office-hours attendance. Raj setting up PostHog next week, self-hosted on the same Fly org to keep costs flat.

[DECISION] Picked Neo4j 5.18 over ArangoDB for relationship traversal on episode graphs. Cypher query ergonomics and APOC procedures outweighed ArangoDB's multi-model edge. Chroma stays as the vector store, Neo4j owns metadata + edges.

[PLAN] Roll out the 21-type classifier in two phases: ship the 12 core types (decision, plan, fact, correction, etc.) next sprint, then add the 9 specialized types (retro, blocker, risk) after we gather 2k labeled episodes from Maya's tagging pass.

[FACT] Composite score formula is 0.45*semantic + 0.25*recency + 0.20*type_boost + 0.10*group_affinity. Recency uses a 30-day half-life exponential. Type boosts are defined in scoring/weights.yaml, reloaded on SIGHUP.

[MEETING] Standup 2024-11-04 with Priya, Dan, and Ramirez: agreed the room/hall/wing metadata maps to project/group/tenant. Dan will backfill wing='system' on the 4,812 legacy episodes currently missing it by Friday.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said ChromaDB holds the edges — wrong. Chroma only stores embeddings + episode_id. All edges (DERIVED_FROM, CONTRADICTS, SUPERSEDES) live in Neo4j. Fixed the architecture diagram in docs/arch.md.

[COMPLETION] Shipped the compaction cron — runs nightly at 03:15 UTC, merges episodes with cosine>0.92 and same room, and demotes score<0.15 to cold storage. First run reduced active set from 38k to 27k episodes.

[HANDOFF] Ramirez taking over the 21-type classifier training loop while I'm on leave. Dataset is at s3://jarvis-mem/labels/v3, fine-tuning script is train_classifier.py, baseline F1 is 0.81 on the held-out 500.

[DECISION] Group_id 'system' is reserved for infra/architecture episodes — Claude and OpenClaw both write there but only the memory-admin role can delete. User-project groups use 'proj_<slug>' format per Priya's naming RFC.

[FACT] ChromaDB collection is 'jarvis_episodes_v2', using text-embedding-3-small (1536 dims). HNSW M=32, ef_construction=200. Persistent client mounted at /var/lib/chroma, backed up hourly to the jarvis-mem-backups bucket.

[PLAN] Next week: add room/hall/wing filters to the retrieval API so callers can scope searches. Hall = conversation thread, room = project, wing = tenant. Default behavior unchanged — searches span all wings the caller has access to.

[COMPLETION] Finished migrating the 21-type classifier from sklearn LogReg to a distilbert-base head. F1 jumped from 0.74 to 0.81, inference still under 40ms on CPU. Old pickle archived at models/legacy/clf_v1.pkl.

[MEETING] Architecture sync with OpenClaw team (Tao, Jin): agreed both agents write to the same Neo4j instance via the shared memory-api service, no direct DB access. Conflict resolution uses last-writer-wins on episode body, union on tags.

[CORRECTION] Compaction threshold was documented as cosine>0.95 but the actual code uses 0.92. Updated README and the runbook. The 0.92 value came from Priya's dedup analysis on the Nov 1 snapshot — keeping it.

[HANDOFF] Passing composite scoring tuning to Dan. Weights live in scoring/weights.yaml, eval harness is bench/score_eval.py, target is nDCG@10 > 0.78 on the golden set. Current baseline is 0.74 with the formula from last Tuesday.

[DECISION] Chose to run Neo4j and ChromaDB as separate pods rather than co-locating. Lets us scale vector ops independently and avoids GC pressure from Chroma's Python client leaking into Neo4j's JVM heap. Ramirez signed off.

[DECISION] Picked launchd over cron for the 6am SOUL.md bootstrap refresh on the Mac Mini. Plist at ~/Library/LaunchAgents/com.openclaw.soulboot.plist, RunAtLoad=true, KeepAlive on crash only. cron kept dropping PATH for the Tailscale CLI.
[FACT] Mac Mini hostname is claw-mini.tailnet-7a2f.ts.net. Tailscale ACL grants dev:maya and dev:jun SSH + port 8081 (MCP bridge). MagicDNS on. Exit node disabled — we route only the agent subnet.

[PLAN] Next sprint: shrink MCP tool surface from 41 tools to ~18. Drop duplicated fs_* wrappers, fold git_status/git_diff into one git_inspect, remove the three Notion tools nobody called in the last 30 days per the usage log.

[COMPLETION] Session handoff file writer shipped. On SIGTERM the agent flushes ~/.openclaw/handoff/latest.json with open tasks, cwd, and last 20 tool calls. Verified across 4 restarts; resume latency 1.2s.

[HANDOFF] Leaving launchd crons half-migrated. com.openclaw.soulboot done, com.openclaw.memcompact still on old cron in /etc/crontab line 14. Maya to finish Monday — plist template in repo at infra/launchd/memcompact.plist.sample.

[MEETING] Standup 10/14 with Jun, Maya, Priya. Agreed SOUL.md stays under 8KB. Priya flagged that bootstrap reads it twice on cold start — Jun owns the fix. Tailscale renewal hits 10/22, Maya has the auth key.

[CORRECTION] Earlier note said launchd job ran every 15 min. Actually StartInterval is 900 seconds but ThrottleInterval clamps to 10, so first run after reboot is delayed. Updated runbook section 3.2.

[DECISION] Agent stays always-on via launchd KeepAlive, not a Docker container. Native Apple Silicon perf matters for the whisper.cpp tool; Docker Desktop eats 1.8GB idle. Reconsider only if we need Linux-only MCP servers.

[FACT] SOUL.md lives at /Users/shared/openclaw/SOUL.md, 6.2KB, sections: identity, tools, guardrails, active_projects. Bootstrap loads it before any tool call. Hash checked against .soul.sha256 — mismatch aborts startup.

[PLAN] Add a tailscale funnel endpoint so Priya can hit MCP bridge from her iPad without installing the client. Cert via Tailscale's LetsEncrypt integration, funnel on :8443, restrict to /mcp/* path prefix.

[COMPLETION] MCP bridge now exposes 23 tools after yesterday's pruning pass. Removed 14 fs duplicates, 3 Notion, 1 broken Linear search. Tool schema cached in ~/.openclaw/tools.json, rebuilt on agent start.

[CORRECTION] The handoff JSON path was wrong in the wiki — it's ~/.openclaw/handoff/latest.json, not ~/openclaw/handoff.json. Fixed wiki page 'Session Resume' and the onboarding doc Maya wrote last week.
[MEETING] 10/16 review with Jun. Decided session handoff should include the active git branch and uncommitted diff stats, not the full diff (too big). Jun prototyping; target PR by Friday.

[FACT] launchd crons for OpenClaw: soulboot (6am daily), memcompact (every 4h), tailscale_healthcheck (every 15m), handoff_gc (sundays 3am). All logs go to /Users/shared/openclaw/logs/ with 7-day logrotate via newsyslog.conf.
